text: Last class I complained politely and complimented the chef.
Today I reflect on the confident traveler I have become.

I used to feel nervous at the airport.
Now I can ask for directions with confidence.
I have learned to adapt when plans change.
I used to plan everything; now I am more spontaneous.
Tuca, after a whole year of travel, how would you say you have changed?
I used to feel nervous about everything. Now I can adapt much more easily.
That is a resilient mindset. What did you learn when plans went wrong?
I have learned to stay calm and be resourceful. I used to panic; now I find another way.
Wonderful. So has all of this been worthwhile for you?
Absolutely. It broadened my world. I am spontaneous now, and I know I can thrive anywhere.
I used to feel nervous, but not anymore.
Now I can travel with confidence.
I stay resilient and resourceful when things go wrong.
The journey was worthwhile; I can thrive anywhere.

Hello, and welcome to our final travel workshop. Over the years, I have guided many learners, and I want to share what I have noticed about confident travelers. At the start, most people are nervous. They do not adapt easily, and a small change in plans feels like a disaster. But little by little, something shifts. A confident traveler has a different mindset. When the train is late or the hotel loses the booking, she stays calm and resilient, and she finds another way. She is resourceful. She is also more spontaneous: she says yes to a last-minute invitation, she tries the strange dish, she talks to strangers. She does not just visit a place; she immerses herself in it, and she lets the trip broaden her view of the world. That is what makes travel truly worthwhile. So my advice for your next journey is simple: embrace the surprises, trust yourself, and you will thrive anywhere. You have everything you need already. Safe travels, and enjoy every moment.

Hi Tuca, it is Sofia. I just listened to your voice note about your trips this year, and honestly, I am so impressed. I remember when you used to be afraid to order food in English, and now you complain politely and compliment the chef like a pro. You really have changed. You used to plan every single minute, and now you are so much more spontaneous and relaxed. The way you described getting lost in that little town and just talking to locals to find your way, that is a real confident traveler. I think this English journey has done something bigger than grammar. It has given you a new mindset. You adapt, you stay resilient when things go wrong, and you actually enjoy the surprises now. I am really proud of you. Let us plan our next adventure together soon, somewhere completely new. Call me back when you can. Bye!

First, at the very beginning, Tuca used to feel nervous and she planned every minute of her trips. Then, lesson by lesson, she learned new words and grammar, and slowly her mindset began to change. After that, she started to adapt when plans went wrong, staying calm and resilient instead of panicking. Next, she became more spontaneous, saying yes to invitations and trying new things to immerse herself in each place. Finally, she realized she had become a confident, resourceful traveler who can thrive anywhere, and she felt the whole journey had been truly worthwhile.

Now I can travel with confidence.
